input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

recent_thinks: [0-2] A blonde woman in a black top stands behind a table featuring a tiered stand of strawberry-topped cakes and a wire tree holding cupcake lollipops. Festive greenery with red berries rests on the table. Large pink and white cupcake cutouts frame the scene on either side against a white background, with a ShinDigZ logo visible.

samples.output: <think>The blonde woman in the black top raises both hands, gesturing with open palms near the wire tree holding cupcake lollipops. Her facial expression transitions from a closed-mouth smile to speaking. The tiered cake stand, festive greenery, and large cupcake cutouts remain stationary.</think><action>silent</action>

compressed_segments.text: A blonde woman in a black top stands behind a table featuring a wire tree of cupcake lollipops and a tiered stand of cakes, framed by large pink and white cupcake cutouts and a ShinDigZ logo. Festive greenery with red berries decorates the table. From 0-10s, she gestures with both hands near the lollipop tree while speaking and smiling. At 10s, she extends her right hand toward the tiered stand on her left. The view cuts to a close-up [12-14s] of the stand, showing cakes in red and white striped wrappers with "Happy Holidays" text, topped with strawberries resembling Santa hats, surrounded by silver ornaments.

[14-18] A close-up shows a white two-tiered stand holding small cakes in red and white striped wrappers with "Happy Holidays" text. The treats are topped with strawberries resembling Santa hats. Festive greenery, red berries, and silver ornaments surround the base, with a blurred black ribbon visible behind. [18-26] The camera cuts to a blonde woman in a black top behind a table. She gestures toward the tiered stand on her left, then rests her hand there while looking at the camera and smiling. A wire tree of lollipops stands on her right, flanked by large pink and white cupcake cutouts with sprinkles.

recent_thinks: [26-28] The blonde woman in the black top stands behind the table with her right hand near the tiered cupcake stand. She closes her mouth, appearing to pause her speech, while facing the camera. The wire lollipop tree and large cupcake cutouts remain static in the background, flanking the table setup with the ShinDigZ logo visible.

[28-30] The blonde woman in the black top continues standing behind the table, smiling and facing the camera. Her right hand remains near the tiered cake stand on the left. The wire lollipop tree and large cupcake cutouts flanking the setup stay static, maintaining the festive display with the ShinDigZ logo visible in the corner.

[30-32] The scene shifts to a close-up of the stand's lower tier, showing four cupcakes in red and white striped wrappers reading "Happy Holidays." Strawberries topped with white frosting sit on each, resembling Santa hats. Silver baubles and pine branches with red berries surround the base, with the ShinDigZ logo visible in the bottom right corner.

[32-34] The camera view tilts upward to reveal the upper tier of the white stand, displaying three more cupcakes in red and white striped wrappers labeled "Happy Holidays." The lower tier remains in view with strawberry-topped treats, while a large blurred black bow becomes visible in the background behind the stand's central column.

[34-36] The camera tilts upward to focus primarily on the top tier of the white stand, showcasing three cupcakes in red and white striped wrappers labeled "Happy Holidays." Each treat features a strawberry topped with white frosting resembling a Santa hat, set against a background with a large blurred black bow.

[36-38] The scene cuts to a wide shot of the blonde woman in a black top standing behind the table. She gestures with her right hand near the tiered stand on her left, while a wire tree of lollipops stands on her right, flanked by large pink and white cupcake cutouts with colorful sprinkles and the ShinDigZ logo.

samples.output: <think>The blonde woman in a black top remains behind the table, gesturing with her right hand near the tiered stand of red and white treats on her left. She faces forward while speaking, flanked by the wire lollipop tree and large cupcake cutouts with the ShinDigZ logo visible.</think><action>silent</action>

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
